nodes.title: Intro Mil Skills&Followership
nodes.description: Development of basic skills of an Army officer, including navigation and communications. Students are exposed to leadership development exercises during leadership laboratories.

nodes.title: Lead Org Ethically&Competently
nodes.description: Identify and resolve ethical dilemmas. Refine counseling and motivating techniques. Examine aspects of tradition and law related to leading as an officer in the Army. Includes a non-credit laboratory to develop, practice and refine leadership skills in a variety of positions. Prerequisite: MS 3241.
nodes.other_requirements: Army# 402; Seniors only; UVM students only; students from affiliate school register for MS 3242 B; Contact instructor for location

nodes.title: Lead&Manage Small Organization
nodes.description: Plan for and adapt to the unexpected in organizations under stress. Examine importance of ethical decisions in a positive climate that enhances team performance. Includes a non-credit laboratory to develop, practice and refine leadership skills in a variety of positions. Prerequisite: MS 2131.
nodes.other_requirements: Army#: 302; Contracted ROTC cadet; Juniors only; UVM students only; students from affiliate school register for MS 2132 B; Contact instructor for location

nodes.title: Ldrshp Challenges&Goal Setting
nodes.description: Plan, conduct, and evaluate activities. Assess organizational cohesion and develop strategies for improvement. Develop confidence in skills to lead people and manage resources. Includes a non-credit laboratory to develop, practice and refine leadership skills in a variety of positions. Prerequisite: MS 2132.
nodes.other_requirements: Army#: 401; Contracted ROTC Cadets/students who have completed MS 2132 and Advanced Camp; UVM students only; Students from affiliate school register for MS 3241 B

nodes.title: Individual&Team Leading
nodes.description: Techniques for training/counseling others as an aspect of continued leadership development. Includes safety and risk management assessments, and planning for individual and team safety. Includes a non-credit laboratory to develop, practice and refine leadership skills in a variety of positions.
nodes.other_requirements: Army#: 202; UVM students only; students from affiliate school register for MS 1220 B; Contact instructor for location

nodes.title: Independent Study
nodes.description: A course which is tailored to fit the interests of a specific student, which occurs outside the traditional classroom/laboratory setting under the supervision of a faculty member, for which credit is awarded. Offered at department discretion.

nodes.title: Leadership&Team Development
nodes.description: Learning and application of ethics-based leadership skills that develop individual abilities and contribute to effective team building. Development of oral presentations, writing, and coordination of group efforts. Includes a non-credit laboratory to develop, practice, and refine leadership skills in a variety of positions.
nodes.other_requirements: UVM students only; Students from affiliate school register for MS 1210 B

nodes.title: Intro to ROTC & US Army
nodes.description: Discussion of the customs, traditions, branches, organization, as well as the many changes in the roles and missions of the Army of the 21st century. Includes a non-credit laboratory to develop, practice and refine leadership skills in a variety of positions.

nodes.title: Teaching Assistantship
nodes.description: Undergraduate student service as a teaching assistant, usually in an introductory level course in the discipline, for which credit is awarded. Offered at department discretion.
nodes.other_requirements: Instructor permission required; Contact instructor for meeting location and time

nodes.title: Lead&Train Small Organizations
nodes.description: Series of opportunities to lead small groups, receive personal assessments, and lead in complex situations. Plan and conduct training to develop leadership skills. Prerequisite: Completion of basic course program or basic camp. Includes a non-credit laboratory to develop, practice and refine leadership skills in a variety of positions.
nodes.other_requirements: Contracted ROTC Cadet/student only; UVM students only; Students from affiliate school register for MS 2131 B
